key_files.reason: Shared render shell for both DM and group bubbles. Houses the compose toolbar (ImageAttachmentButton + Textarea + IconButton). The emoji trigger button and picker popover both live here. The message bubble slot (lines 396-423) is where reaction badges attach.

Group chat send path and inbound applicationMessage dispatch. The kind-dispatch switch (line 124) is where a case for reaction kind must be added. Optimistic send pattern with tempId (lines 177-197) is the template for reaction optimistic UI.

Inbound rumor kind dispatch for groups (lines 530-643). The if/else-if chain at line 534+ is where a reaction kind branch slots in. sendRumorSafe+buildRumor helpers at lines 72-110 are the canonical pattern for group outbound publishing.
idb-keyval persistence layer for messages. Key pattern quizzl:messages:{groupId}, serialized append queue. Reactions table must follow the same key convention and queue pattern.

DM outbound publish path: signDirectMessage at line 126 builds+signs kind-4, publishDirectMessage at line 149 publishes. Reaction kind-7 for DMs must follow the same sign-then-publish, NIP-04 encrypted payload pattern.

DM inbound ingestion: ingestEvent (line 60) decrypts and produces ChatMessage. Outbound sendMessage (line 144) demonstrates real-event-id optimistic pattern for DMs. Reaction send for DMs mirrors both.

Closest existing model for a secondary IDB table alongside messages. The per-key write-queue pattern (lines 67-78) and key prefix quizzl:polls:{groupId} are exactly the shape the reactions table should replicate.

Template for a new rumor-kind serialise/parse pair (POLL_OPEN_KIND=10, POLL_VOTE_KIND=11, POLL_CLOSE_KIND=12). A reaction kind (e.g. kind 7 within MLS application messages) would follow the same module shape.
Pattern for a single IconButton in the ChatBox toolbar. The emoji trigger button must mimic this: size=sm, variant=ghost, IconButton wrapper, no state in the button itself.

subscribeToGroupMessages — the live and historical kind-445 subscription that calls onApplicationMessage for all decrypted group rumors. The resolver callback is the only entry point for new inbound rumor kinds.

1_message_bubble_rendering.summary: Both DM and group surfaces render through a single shared component: ChatBox.tsx. GroupChat.tsx (line 12) is a thin wrapper that reads from useChatStore and passes props down. ContactChat.tsx (line 215) directly instantiates ChatBox.
1_message_bubble_rendering.bubble_slot: Each message is a <Flex direction='column'> at ChatBox.tsx:396. The inner <Box> at line 413 wraps the bubble itself (text, image, poll, announcement). There is currently NO row below the bubble for badges — the Flex column ends after renderStructuredMessage. Reaction badges would be inserted as a new child of the column <Flex direction='column'> immediately after the <Box> at line 413.

accessory_rows_already_present.note: Rendered only for the first message in a group (grouped=false). Direction flips per isSelf.
ImageMessageBubble replaces the text box in renderStructuredMessage.
Structured messages of type poll_open/poll_close/invite_cancelled replace the text bubble with a dedicated component.

2_compose_area_input.summary: The compose area is a fixed <Flex p={2} gap={2} align='flex-end'> at ChatBox.tsx:510-539.

toolbar_children_left_to_right.note: Auto-grow, rows=1, maxH=128px, resize=none. Handles Enter-to-send and paste-image.

2_compose_area_input.emoji_trigger_slot: A new IconButton matching the ImageAttachmentButton pattern (size=sm, variant=ghost) would be inserted between ImageAttachmentButton (line 511) and the Textarea (line 512) — OR between Textarea and Send to keep attachment affordances left-aligned.
2_compose_area_input.image_attachment_button_pattern: ImageAttachmentButton.tsx:10 — IconButton wraps a hidden <input type=file>. Emoji trigger would be simpler: just an IconButton toggling a popover/portal state.

3_outbound_dm_publishing.summary: DMs use kind 4 (NIP-04 symmetric encryption, NOT NIP-17/59 gift wrap). The payload is a JSON blob encrypted with nip04.encrypt.

sign_function.name: signDirectMessage
sign_function.note: Builds payload via buildPayload, encrypts via encryptDirectPayload (nip04.encrypt), creates NDKEvent kind=4 with ['p', peerPubkeyHex] tag, calls event.sign()

publish_function.name: publishDirectMessage
publish_function.note: Calls signDirectMessage then event.publish(). Returns event.id.

ndk_client.note: connectNdk(privateKeyHex) is called before signing in ContactChat.tsx:151

3_outbound_dm_publishing.reaction_kind7_slot: A reaction for DMs would call signDirectMessage (or a new signDirectReaction helper) with kind=7 instead of kind=4, content=emoji, and a ['e', targetEventId] tag — or encrypted similarly if NIP-04 wrap is used. The existing encryptDirectPayload/nip04.encrypt path is the model. ContactChat.sendMessage at line 144 is the pattern to copy for fire-and-forget reaction publish.
3_outbound_dm_publishing.note_on_nip17: The codebase uses kind 4 (NIP-04), NOT kind 14/13/59 gift-wrap. DIRECT_MESSAGE_KIND is hardcoded to 4 at directMessages.ts:7. The spec's mention of kind-14/13 does not match the current transport.

4_outbound_mls_group_publishing.summary: Group messages use marmot-ts sendApplicationRumor, wrapped by buildRumor + sendRumorSafe in MarmotContext.

build_rumor.name: buildRumor
build_rumor.signature: (kind: number, content: string, pubkey: string, tags: string[][]) => rumor
build_rumor.note: Uses getEventHash from applesauce-core to compute the id field. This is the canonical rumor constructor.

send_rumor_safe.name: sendRumorSafe
send_rumor_safe.note: Calls group.sendApplicationRumor(rumor). Retries up to MAX_RETRIES=3 after committing unapplied proposals. softFail option available for non-critical sends.

chat_send_via_context.name: sendChatSafe
chat_send_via_context.note: Calls group.sendChatMessage(content). This is a higher-level wrapper that does not use buildRumor — it exists for the simple text path only. Reactions must use buildRumor+sendRumorSafe directly (as profile/score/poll updates do).

4_outbound_mls_group_publishing.reaction_slot: A new reaction send would call buildRumor(REACTION_KIND, emoji, pubkeyHex, [['e', targetMessageId]]) then sendRumorSafe(mlsGroup, rumor).

dm_decrypt.name: ingestEvent
dm_decrypt.note: Calls decryptDirectPayload (nip04.decrypt). Returns ChatMessage or null. NDK subscription events at lines 121-122 call this handler. No kind dispatch — all kind-4 events are treated as chat messages.

group_decrypt_and_dispatch.note: The onApplicationMessage callback in subscribeToGroupMessages receives already-decrypted rumors. Kind dispatch is an if/else-if chain: SCORE_RUMOR_KIND (line 534), PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND (line 541), CHAT_MESSAGE_KIND=9 (line 564), POLL_OPEN_KIND=10 (line 589), POLL_VOTE_KIND=11 (line 606), POLL_CLOSE_KIND=12 (line 625). A reaction kind case is inserted here.

group_subscription_entry_point.name: subscribeToGroupMessages
group_subscription_entry_point.note: EpochResolver.ingestEvent processes kind-445 events. Decrypted application messages are emitted via onApplicationMessage callback. No kind filtering here — all kinds reach the callback.

react_layer_handoff.group: MarmotContext bumps chatVersion counter (line 585) after appendMessage; ChatStoreContext re-reads IDB on chatVersion change (lines 159-169). The applicationMessage event on the MarmotGroup object (ChatStoreContext.tsx:146) is also a live path for self-sent messages.
react_layer_handoff.dm: ContactChat upsertMessages (line 52) merges into React state from NDK subscription or ingest batch. No external bus — direct setState.

gift_wrap_for_dm.name: unwrapGiftWrap
gift_wrap_for_dm.note: NIP-59 two-layer decrypt (gift wrap kind 1059 -> seal kind 13 -> rumor). Used for MLS Welcome messages and join requests. NOT used for DMs (those are plain kind-4).

storage_primitives.note: Only IndexedDB library in use. Both custom createStore() named stores and the default store are used.
Used for settings, user profile, identity keys, unread timestamps, processed gift-wrap IDs. Not for messages or reactions.

message_store.write_pattern: serialized promise queue (appendQueues Map at line 42) — one queue per key, prevents concurrent write races

poll_store.write_pattern: same serialized queue pattern (writeQueues Map at line 67)

media_store.note: Named createStore() stores for large blobs

6_persistence_layer.reactions_table_recommendation_suppresse: Reactions should follow pollPersistence.ts pattern: quizzl:reactions:{groupId} in the default store, per-key write queue, clearAllReactions scans by key prefix.

clearAllData_integration.note: clearAccountScopedIdbData runs all clear tasks with Promise.allSettled. Reactions clear must be added here.

7_identity_scoping.summary: Active identity is a single keypair stored in localStorage, loaded by NostrIdentityContext.

fields.pubkeyHex: NostrIdentityContext line 39 — hex public key, used as senderPubkey in ChatMessage
fields.privateKeyHex: NostrIdentityContext line 41 — passed to connectNdk and signing functions
fields.seedHex: NostrIdentityContext line 43 — 128-bit seed for BIP-39 backup

consumed_by.note: pubkeyHex and privateKeyHex passed as props from the contacts page
pubkey prop identifies self in optimistic entries
useNostrIdentity() — pubkeyHex threaded into buildRumor and sendRumorSafe

7_identity_scoping.reaction_identity_scoping: The spec's identity_id for scoping reaction rows is pubkeyHex. chatPersistence already scopes by groupId (not by pubkeyHex). DM store uses dm:{peerPubkeyHex} as groupId. Reactions should scope the same way: reactions are per-thread (groupId), not per-identity globally.
7_identity_scoping.note_no_multi_identity: No multi-identity support. There is exactly one active keypair at a time.

group_chat_optimistic.pattern: tempId = crypto.randomUUID(), optimistic ChatMessage added to state before await sendChatSafe. On failure, filter out by tempId. No real-id reconciliation for group messages — the real rumor id is never assigned to the optimistic entry; the real entry arrives via the applicationMessage event callback (line 120-140) and is deduplicated by id in setMessages.
group_chat_optimistic.caveat: Group optimistic entries keep their tempId permanently unless the inbound live rumor matches. This means a sent group message can briefly show twice (tempId version + rumor version) — the setMessages dedup at line 135 prevents that for the live rumor.

dm_chat_optimistic.pattern: For text DMs: sign first to get the real event.id before publish, insert optimistic under real id immediately. On failure, filter out by event.id. NDK echo race is avoided because tempId === realId from the start (see comment at line 148).

for_image_dm.pattern: tempId = crypto.randomUUID(), then after upload+publish, swap tempId for result.eventId via filter+splice at line 200-203.

8_optimistic_ui_patterns.reaction_optimistic_shape: Reactions should use DM-style real-id-first for DMs (sign first, get id, insert, publish) and tempId-then-drop for groups (insert under tempId, remove on failure, let live rumor insert the canonical entry).

9_hover_long_press_affordances.summary: No hover or long-press affordances exist on message bubbles in ChatBox.tsx. The only _hover styles are on the new-messages badge (line 444) and the image-retry button (line 503). There are no onContextMenu handlers, no per-bubble menus, no copy/delete/reply actions.
9_hover_long_press_affordances.implication: The emoji reaction trigger must introduce the first bubble-level interactive affordance. There is no existing pattern to follow for hover menus on bubbles — this is net-new UX for the project.

closest_pattern.note: The new-messages badge uses position=sticky, bottom=3 inside the scroll container, with _hover={{ opacity: 0.9 }} — the only precedent for an overlay element within the message list area.

patterns.name: buildRumor + sendRumorSafe for group outbound
patterns.description: All non-chat group application messages (score, profile, poll) use buildRumor(kind, content, pubkey) then sendRumorSafe(mlsGroup, rumor) with optional softFail. getEventHash from applesauce-core computes the rumor id.

patterns.name: pollSync-style kind module
patterns.description: Each rumor kind has a paired serialise/parse module (scoreSync.ts, profileSync.ts, pollSync.ts). The module exports KIND constant, serialiseX, and parseX with null-on-invalid returns.

patterns.name: idb-keyval serialized write queue
patterns.description: Every persistent store that may receive concurrent writes uses a per-key promise chain (appendQueues / writeQueues Map) to serialize concurrent appends and prevent lost updates.

patterns.name: chatVersion / pollVersion counter bump pattern
patterns.description: MarmotContext holds monotonically increasing version counters. After IDB write succeeds, it bumps the counter (.then(() => setChatVersion(v => v+1))). Consumers (ChatStoreContext, PollStoreContext) re-read IDB in a useEffect on the counter change.

patterns.name: IconButton size=sm variant=ghost for toolbar accessory
patterns.description: Every non-send toolbar button is an IconButton with size=sm, variant=ghost. The send button uses colorScheme=brand. New emoji trigger must match this.

patterns.name: tempId optimistic + inbound dedup for group messages
patterns.description: Group send inserts a crypto.randomUUID() optimistic entry, then the real rumor arrives via applicationMessage callback and is deduplicated by id in setMessages. No explicit swap.

patterns.name: real-event-id optimistic for DM text messages
patterns.description: DM text send signs first (getting the real id), inserts under the real id optimistically, then publishes. Eliminates the NDK echo duplicate race entirely.

conventions.convention: All user-facing strings go through useCopy() / i18n.ts Copy type
conventions.evidence: ChatBox.tsx:95 copy = useCopy(); strings like copy.groups.chatLoading at line 343

conventions.convention: Dynamic routes use query params not path segments
conventions.evidence: CLAUDE.md: 'Use query parameters instead of path segments for client-side dynamic data'

conventions.convention: IDB key prefix quizzl: for default store entries
Named idb-keyval stores (createStore) for large/separate domains
clearAllX functions scan by key prefix and use delMany for atomic batch delete
MLS application message kinds are small integers; chat=9, score=1, profile=6, poll=10-12
DM transport is kind-4 NIP-04, not NIP-17/59 gift-wrap
conventions.evidence: directMessages.ts:7 DIRECT_MESSAGE_KIND=4, encryptDirectPayload uses nip04.encrypt at line 108
